issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason:
CORRECTION to my last note (I verified the build this time instead of inferring). I previously said "your release build sets FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS=true so users see coach-build." That is WRONG — I checked the actual compiled flags in the v148 release AND the OTA web bundle (dist): FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS = FALSE in both. It is set in no .env, so it defaults off. You turned it off yourself via i-20260620-1469 ("remove the How-should-we-build-your-plan choice, route straight into the builders").

What this means for fd1e:
- The "Build Your Meals Plan / Coach Kabir / Building your meals..." screen in your screenshot ONLY renders when DEFER_BUILDERS is ON. The current release (v147/v148) has it OFF -> users go straight into the ALWAYS-CUSTOM builder (you build workout + diet, finalize self-heals). So that exact "Building your meals..." coach-build screen does NOT appear for anyone on the updated app.
- Your screenshot (06-25) is almost certainly from an older build still installed on your device that predates the 06-20 flag flip. A fresh install / update will not show it.

So fd1e's specific screen is effectively retired in the current release. Two honest options for you:
1) If always-custom is what you want (per 1469), fd1e's coach-build screen is moot for new installs -> we can close it. The remaining "long wait" lives in the always-custom DIET builder's AI meal-generation (suggestMealCached, 4 slots) when you tap build there; I can apply the same instant-template-first-fill fix to THAT surface instead.
2) If you actually want the "Coach builds it all" one-tap path back, re-enable DEFER_BUILDERS (set VITE_FEATURE_DEFER_BUILDERS=true in .env.production) and I'll ship the template-first-fill fix on that screen.

Either way the wait-fix is ready; I just need to know which path you want as the default. Apologies for the earlier wrong inference — verified now.

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Onboarding pre-selects Gender=Male and never requires a choice; gender drives BMR (biological_sex uncollected -> fallback), so non-male users who do not change it get male-BMR calorie targets (~166 kcal high).
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason:
S6 first-time-UX walk (fresh account, current always-custom release). Flow is otherwise clean (0 console errors, clear gating helpers, good screens). One precision/data-quality concern worth your call:

FINDING: The Basics step PRE-SELECTS Gender=Male (OnboardingFlow.tsx:256 inits gender:"male") and Continue gates only on NAME — line 714 never marks gender missing because it already holds a valid default. Gender is "identity-only since v5", BUT BMR still uses biological_sex ?? gender (onboarding-calc.ts:48; PlanReadyOverview.tsx:181), and biological_sex is NEVER collected in the condensed onboarding (stays null, line 257). So gender is the EFFECTIVE BMR sex.

IMPACT: a female/other first-timer who does not change the pre-selected Male gets a male-BMR calorie target (~166 kcal too high, Mifflin-St Jeor sex term). For a precision-branded app this silently skews non-male users targets.

OPTIONS (your product/UX call): (a) do NOT pre-select gender + gate Continue on an explicit choice (most accurate; small flow change); (b) collect biological_sex explicitly (the v5 design intent) so BMR does not depend on the identity-gender fallback; (c) keep the default if acceptable for onboarding speed.

I did NOT silently change it — it touches shipped onboarding gating + the v5 biological_sex design, so it is your decision.
